description: Alerting rules for the Azure Data Exfiltration Chain scenario (Defender, Blob Storage, NSG)

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Azure Data Exfil Chain — Defender Exfiltration Alert
rules.investigationGuide:
# Microsoft Defender Exfiltration Finding

The cloud threat detector flagged an exfiltration action — highest-fidelity signal.

## Triage
1. Identify principal: `FROM logs-azure.defender-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND azure.defender.alert_type LIKE "*Exfiltration*" | KEEP @timestamp, azure.defender.alert_type, azure.defender.severity, source.ip, destination.ip | SORT @timestamp DESC`
2. Check if principal still has active sessions
3. Rotate credentials immediately if confirmed

## Escalate when
- Always page security on-call for Exfiltration findings
- Principal has access to customer-data storage

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Azure Data Exfil Chain — Large Blob Read
rules.investigationGuide:
# Blob Read With High Result Count

A principal performed many blob read operations programmatically.

## Triage
1. Identify who and what: `FROM logs-azure.activitylogs-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND event.action == "Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers/blobs/read" AND azure.activitylogs.result_count > 100 | KEEP @timestamp, user.name, source.ip, user_agent.original, azure.activitylogs.resource_id | SORT azure.activitylogs.result_count DESC`
2. Is the container sensitive (PII, secrets, backups)?
3. Console or programmatic? Check user_agent

## Escalate when
- Container holds customer data or secrets
- Principal is a service account reading at unusual volume

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Azure Data Exfil Chain — NSG Outbound Flow
rules.investigationGuide:
# NSG Flow Logs High Egress Bytes

A single flow shipped >50 MiB outbound.

## Triage
1. Top sources: `FROM logs-azure.nsgflowlogs-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND network.direction == "egress" AND azure.nsgflowlogs.bytes > 52428800 | STATS bytes = SUM(azure.nsgflowlogs.bytes) BY source.ip | SORT bytes DESC | LIMIT 25`
2. Check destination geo — foreign IPs are higher signal
3. Cross-reference with Microsoft Defender rule

## Escalate when
- Same source IP triggering repeatedly
- Destination on egress-deny list

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Azure Data Exfil Chain — Full Chain Correlation
rules.investigationGuide:
# Full Chain Correlation — Data Exfiltration

**Treat as confirmed incident until proven otherwise.** Multiple exfiltration signals co-occurred.

## Triage
1. Page on-call security lead immediately
2. Correlate: `FROM logs-azure.defender-*, logs-azure.blob_storage-*, logs-azure.network_security_groups-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 30m | WHERE (event.dataset == "azure.defender" AND azure.defender.alert_type LIKE "*Exfiltration*") OR (event.dataset == "azure.blob_storage" AND azure.blob_storage.bytes_out > 52428800) OR (event.dataset == "azure.network_security_groups" AND azure.network_security_groups.direction == "Outbound") | KEEP @timestamp, event.dataset, user.name, source.ip | SORT @timestamp ASC`
3. Contain: rotate credentials, block destination IP

## Escalate when
- **Always.** This is the page-the-CISO bar.
